parsed_review.summary: CP00-803 is a descriptor-only, no-runtime, no-real-data closeout pack for RP26 Enterprise SaaS Hardening (RP26.P05.M04.S13-RP26.P05.M06.S08, 40 units, risk B). The pack-level claim is supported and contains no runtime/enterprise-trust overclaim: the manifest declares production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, status=in_progress, production_ready_descriptor_verified=false, and the production_ready_flag (enterprise_saas_fixture_permission_audit_transition_descriptor_verified) is an explicitly descriptor-verified flag only. Independent Read/Grep/Glob verification confirms: (1) Identity/coverage agree across the registry binding (ENTERPRISE_SAAS_CP803_PACK_BINDING, registry.js:307), the manifest plan_binding_snapshot, the live closeout-pack-plan.json packs[CP00-803] (line 398, the first generated pack, deviation_from_plan=false, within_default_risk_range=true), and the contract current_pack/latest_pack/latest_projection (CP00-803) — same range, risk B, unit_count 40, next CP00-804/RP26.P05.M06.S09, ledger SHA 13c7ed65. The live plan DOES contain CP00-803, so the validate-rp26 script manifest-snapshot fallback is not exercised. (2) ENTERPRISE_SAAS_CP803_REQUIREMENTS deliverable_counts {implementation:14,fixture:14,test:6,hermes_evidence:2,claude_review:2,security_audit:2}=40 and micro_title_row_counts {Secondary Workflow Slice:10,Permission And Audit Binding:22,Synthetic Fixture Set:8} match the prompt exactly; FIXTURE_GOLDEN_ROWS (22 entries, registry.js:539) slices M04=slice(12,22)->10, M05=full->22, M06=slice(0,8)->8 map in-order to the planned unit titles (S13-S22, S01-S22, S01-S08); validateCoverage and three model.test.js CP803 tests (lines 654-711) enforce the distributions, row counts (22 binding rows, 8 synthetic rows), and contract promotion. (3) freezeResult (service.js:47) merges the full ENTERPRISE_SAAS_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION (40+ false flags, registry.js:324) into every descriptor/case-set/packet, and createCaseSet (service.js:211) builds every row descriptor_only=true / runtime_execution=false with raw/tenant/sso/scim/secret payload flags false plus all-false ROW_EXTRAS; validators.js validateDescriptor (lines 142-203) independently rejects any row that opens identity runtime, creates/allows cross-tenant routes, generates/exposes secret/key material, evaluates/writes permission decisions, writes audit events/bodies, executes UI/API, loads real tenant data, or claims enterprise trust. SSO/SCIM descriptors (sso.js/scim.js) remain descriptor-only with assertion/payload/secret exposure flags false. (4) Authority boundary holds: descriptor.authority_boundary sets claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true (runtime_opening_pack_id=CP00-804); claude_packet read_only=true, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, blocks_pack_on_p0_p1_p2=true. (5) Hermes is used only as a deterministic descriptor evidence packet/receipt (createEnterpriseSaasCp803HermesEvidencePacket: writes_product_state=false, emits_runtime_receipt=false, receipt_shape descriptor_only_enterprise_saas_no_write), not product runtime. (6) The committed contract promotes CP00-803 to current/latest_pack and latest_projection (EnterpriseSaasCp803FixturePermissionAuditTransitionDescriptor, contract line 24307) with prior packs CP788-CP802 preserved as historical (15 projections) and top-level validation.valid=true/errors=[] (lines 29947-29949); validateEnterpriseSaasHardeningContract and scripts/validate-rp26-enterprise-saas-hardening-contract.mjs enforce schema, RP26 program, latest pack/projection identity, mandatory artifacts, no-leak guards, capabilities, safety gates, and research alignment. (7) Local-validation evidence is clean: validation-report.json (prompt 2508<25600 bytes, no full diff/source embedded, read_tools_used and allowed, summary_only prohibited and not used, schema present, no forbidden tools, no omitted active untracked files) and readiness-report.json (ready, auth_preflight exit 0 with well-formed JSON result, no errors/warnings). (8) CP178+ hardening: pack_level_claude_review has status=review_pending with valid_review_receipts=[] and invalid_review_attempts=[] (manifest lines 522-525) — no prior review evidence requiring auth-failure/0-byte/malformed-JSON/fenced/tool-shaped/missing-field rejection; this review's raw-output/receipt are produced downstream. (9) index.js wildcard-re-exports registry/service/validators (full CP803 surface); npm test includes packages/enterprise/test/*.test.js; all nine expected npm validation scripts exist in package.json; changed-file-scope active refs are the seven enterprise files with unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room, ldip-integration invalid-attempt, workbook docs) correctly preserved/excluded and active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted empty. One non-blocking P3: downstream closeout artifacts (command-evidence.json, claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are not yet present, which is expected at this pre-review stage and identical to the posture recorded by the already-closed CP00-802. Claude remains a read-only, non-final reviewer; final human approval for enterprise trust/runtime opening is correctly retained outside this receipt.

findings.title: Downstream closeout artifacts not yet generated (expected at pre-review stage)
findings.details: required_pack_artifacts lists manifest.json, command-evidence.json, claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, but only manifest.json currently exists in docs/closeout-packs/cp00-803/. This is consistent with a mid-closeout pack whose pack_level_claude_review.status is 'review_pending' with valid_review_receipts=[] and invalid_review_attempts=[] (manifest lines 522-525): claude-review-result.json and adjudication.md are produced by/after this very review, and command-evidence.json (recording the npm validation command outputs) and construction-inspection.json are produced at closeout finalization. The immediately prior pack CP00-802 (already closed) contains all five artifacts and its review receipt recorded the identical pre-review condition as a single non-blocking P3, confirming this is sequencing rather than a defect. Because these are downstream of the review, the 'adjudication' and 'inspection' inputs and the actual test/validation command-execution results named in the review focus cannot be independently verified from within this receipt; however the implementation, tests (source read and asserting correct coverage/boundaries), contract, Hermes/Claude packets, local review-harness validation evidence, and no-write/no-real-data boundaries were all independently verified and pass, and the manifest does not yet claim production_ready=true so there is no overclaim to block.
findings.recommendation: No action required for this review. Ensure npm run closeout-pack:validate (which enforces required_pack_artifacts) and the full validation command set (closeout-pack-plan:validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements/weighted/fullplan/goal:closeout/rp00:control-plane validators, git diff --check) are run and pass before the final CP00-803 closeout commit, so command-evidence.json, claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json are present and adjudicated prior to flipping production_ready and committing.
findings.post_review_disposition: Expected/non-blocking. Artifacts are generated by this review plus the closeout finalization step and enforced by the closeout-pack:validate gate before commit; does not block pack or goal closeout.
